nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; found at depths of 12–140 m, frequently over soft bottoms near reef structures

diet.notes: Tends to accept frozen meaty preparations and algae-based foods in captivity. Naturally feeds on small invertebrates and has been documented eating Aiptasia anemones. Features a specialized elongated snout with fine hairlike teeth suited for picking food from crevices; some individuals may need targeted feeding with varied offerings to become fully conditioned.

summary: A striking Eastern Atlantic butterflyfish with a silvery-yellow body and two bold black bars, generally considered intermediate in difficulty; best kept in a fish-only or FOWLR system with ample open swimming space.
careNotes:
Marcella Butterflyfish have been described as needing plenty of room to swim and are generally advised for fish-only with live rock (FOWLR) setups, as they tend to nip coral polyps and small ornamental invertebrates and are not considered reef-safe. They can be kept singly or as a mated pair, but have been known to show aggression toward other Prognathodes species and similarly-patterned butterflyfishes.

Feeding: specimens generally accept frozen meaty foods (mysis, krill, chopped marine flesh) and algae-based preparations. Their specialized elongated snout and fine teeth allow them to pick food from rockwork crevices; varied offerings and spot-feeding with a turkey baster may help condition newly acquired individuals.

Temperature note: wild specimens have been recorded at depths of 12–140 m, where temperatures can dip to around 18°C; aquarium care sources advise a narrower range of 22–26°C as more appropriate for captive maintenance.

Purchasing as an already-bonded pair when possible is advisable.

The species is also sold under the trade names Diamond Butterflyfish and French Butterflyfish.
breedingNotes: The Marcella Butterflyfish is reported to form pairs during breeding in the wild, and spawning is oviparous (eggs are released and fertilized in the water column). Captive breeding has not been documented in the aquarium hobby literature.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (11.6 cm TL vs LiveAquaria 5 in/12.7 cm — close agreement), temperature range (FishBase wild range 18.3–25.9°C vs LiveAquaria aquarium recommendation 22.2–25.6°C — diverge at lower end, averaged upper end to 26°C, used LiveAquaria minimum for aquarium guidance), native range (Eastern Atlantic, Senegal to Angola), taxonomy, and breeding behavior (forms pairs, oviparous).

fish.breedingNotes: No captive breeding has been documented. The species forms pairs in the wild and is oviparous; its deep-water collection range (12–140 m) makes wild-caught specimens the likely only source available to the hobby.
